description_long: This object is a robust, cylindrical kettle with a slightly flared rim and a thick, ringed base, giving it a sturdy appearance. The surface is matte and dark gray, featuring visible scratches and wear marks that suggest frequent use or age. A large, side-mounted handle with a squared-off, arched shape is attached, providing a secure grip for lifting or pouring. The kettle lacks a visible spout, and there is no discernible lid or opening mechanism on the top, indicating a simple, utilitarian design. The overall form is reminiscent of traditional metal tankards or mugs, with a utilitarian aesthetic suitable for urban or industrial settings. The object appears to be made primarily of metal, likely steel or iron, with a hollow interior for holding liquid.
description: A sturdy, cylindrical metal kettle with a thick ringed base, matte gray finish, and a large arched side handle. The surface is worn and scratched, with no visible spout or lid.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cylindrical body, flared rim, and the full profile of the arched handle on the right.
description_view_1: Rear view dominated by the handle, which is centered and extends vertically along the back of the kettle.
description_view_2: Side view with the handle fully visible on the left, showing the smooth, continuous body and the ringed base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with no handle or spout visible, highlighting the plain, uninterrupted cylindrical surface.
